official.name: Elizabeth Warren
official.office_address: 311 Hart Senate Office Building Washington DC 20510

summary.narrative: This term, Senator Warren's record on her campaign 'Plan to Fight Back' commitments shows consistent, sustained legislative and oversight activity: she filed budget amendments, opposed nominees through tough questioning, sponsored numerous bills (including a wealth tax and corporate-tax measures), and issued anti-corruption resolutions and investigation demands. Most of these efforts reached the introduction or floor-advocacy stage but, as a minority-party member, did not result in enacted law. Her negative commitments split: the ACA was not repealed during the period covered, while cited sources indicate billionaire-favoring tax legislation was enacted despite her opposition.
summary.grade_rationale: The pattern is high activity and consistent follow-through on oversight and legislative sponsorship (multiple D2-D3 outcomes), constrained by minority status that kept most measures in committee or as floor opposition. Promise quality is mixed — several directional 'fight' pledges with low clarity alongside specific, measurable commitments like the wealth tax — and one negative promise (oppose billionaire tax cuts) shows the condition occurring despite her opposition.

data_gaps: Many qualifying promises derive from a single TIER-1 campaign-era op-ed (c23) and the 2024 campaign video (c25/c22/c29); supporting campaign-video items are mostly TIER-3 reprints of the same announcement. Delivery evidence is strong TIER-1 (congress.gov sponsored bills, Senate press releases). The 'oppose billionaire tax cuts' D0 rests on cited references to enactment of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (c194); reviewer should confirm enactment date and scope. Several 'fight every fight' pledges are directional (clarity 2) and capped at M1 on magnitude grounds. Wealth tax bill enactment status should be re-checked closer to term end.

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 24.4% elapsed (as of 2026-06-22). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
